commit.message:
Remove video system, corruption mechanic, and glitch effects

- Remove VideoOverlay component, video triggers, and all video references
- Remove applyRandomCorruption, recover command, core dump spread
- Remove corruption display (DATA LOSS, CORRUPTION markers)
- Remove glitch visual effects (CSS keyframes, state, useEffect)
- Remove GLITCH_DURATIONS and GLITCH_TIMING constants
- Remove videosShownThisRun from GameState and saves
- Remove corruptible/corruptedLines/truncatedLine from types
- Remove glitchy streaming mode (replaced with slow)
- Delete public/videos/ directory and video test files
- Clean up all related test assertions (1138 tests pass)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Bigger blog cards + tighter section spacing

- Blog cards: bigger padding (1.1→1.4rem), title 0.92→1rem, description 160 chars + 3 lines
- Description shows on mobile too (was hidden)
- Section padding: 4rem → 2rem (less wasted space between sections)
- Section header margin: 2rem → 1.2rem

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Playwright browser install hang: auto-answer npx prompt

npx prompts 'Ok to proceed? (y)' when installing @playwright/cli for
the first time. Since RunInstallCommandAsync redirects stdout/stderr,
the prompt was invisible and the TE agent hung indefinitely during init.

Fix: Add --yes flag to npx command, redirect stdin and auto-write 'y'
as a belt-and-suspenders approach.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Building Blocks of Transformers series: 7 extensive blog posts with interactive animations

- Single-Head Attention: QKV framework, scaled dot-product, step-through animation
- Multi-Head Attention: split-attend-concat, head specialization, parallel heads animation
- Feed-Forward Network: expand-compress, ReLU/GELU/SwiGLU, key-value memory interpretation
- Transformer Block: residual connections, pre-norm vs post-norm, full data flow animation
- Positional Encoding: sinusoidal formula, RoPE, ALiBi, interactive heatmap
- Layer Normalization: LayerNorm vs RMSNorm, bar-chart normalization animation
- Embedding Layer: lookup table, weight tying, token-to-vector animation

Also updates blog.js navigation (adds building-blocks, removes attention section)
and transformers index.html to feature building blocks as Part 1.

commit.message:
Add Embedding Layer blog post (Building Blocks of Transformers Part 7)

Comprehensive deep dive covering:
- Token-to-vector lookup table mechanism
- One-hot encoding comparison
- Vocabulary and tokenization connection with model comparison table
- Embedding dimension tradeoffs across model scales
- Interactive SVG animation showing step-by-step token lookup
- sqrt(d_model) scaling explanation
- Weight tying (shared embeddings) with benefits and model list
- Embedding space geometry and analogies
- Initialization strategies (normal, truncated, Xavier, pretrained)
- Segment and token type embeddings (BERT vs GPT)
- Parameter count analysis across model sizes
- Clean pseudo-code implementation

* fix(codegen): reject overflowing msgpack u32 fields

Add msgpack wire coverage for program metadata fields and reject\noverflowing u32 metadata at the Rust↔C++ boundary.\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(astgen): preserve checked u32 msgpack parsing

Restore the generator-side uint32 overflow guard used by the msgpack
reader so regeneration does not silently replace fail-closed parsing with
unchecked truncation.

Route generated u32 field and vector reads through the shared checked helper,
make the special-cased schema_version parser use the same path, and regenerate
the checked-in reader from the updated source of truth.

* feat(build): add host preparation, Rust bridge generation, and native host build selection

Step 8 Part 4: Integrate host preparation into the build pipeline.

  cxx-qt bridge code matching the native workspace patterns

commit.message:
fix: add minimum frame to stats window to prevent layout loop on macOS 26 Tahoe (#33) (#34)

NavigationSplitView(.balanced) requires a concrete lower-bound width
during its first-responder layout pass (NSWindow.layoutIfNeeded).
Without it, AppKit on macOS 26 Tahoe re-enters
NSPerformVisuallyAtomicChange recursively, deadlocking the main thread.

Add .frame(minWidth: 600, minHeight: 400) to CompanionWindowView inside
the 'stats' Window scene. 600pt = 180pt sidebar min + 420pt detail,
matching the existing navigationSplitViewColumnWidth(min: 180) constraint.

The other two Window scenes ('session-complete', 'coach-intervention')
already carry .windowResizability(.contentSize) and are unaffected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(#137,#138): auto-scroll during streaming + placeholder title ellipsis fix (#140)

* docs: add #96, #103, #110 to ROADMAP.md with correct phases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#137,#138): auto-scroll during streaming + placeholder title for short messages

- ChatScreen: add LaunchedEffect(isGenerating) with snapshotFlow to scroll during
  streaming; only scrolls if user is near the bottom (lastIndex - lastVisible <= 2)
- ChatViewModel: always append ellipsis to placeholder title so session-restore
  heuristic (endsWith '...') reliably fires for short first messages too

Closes #137
Closes #138

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#139): backtick-wrapped URLs render as clickable links not code spans

The model frequently wraps bare URLs in backticks. The inline code regex ran
before URL detection, so the CODE span (starting at the backtick) won
deduplication and the URL appeared as monospace code. Fix: detect when a
code span's trimmed content starts with http://, https://, or www. and emit
a URL span instead.

Closes #139

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address code review issues on PR #140

- ChatScreen: use rememberUpdatedState so snapshotFlow tracks live state
  across recompositions (was capturing stale ref — scroll never fired)
  cancelling the animated scroll triggered on new message insertion
  so mixed-content code spans (e.g. backtick-url-with-text) stay as CODE
  rather than emitting a broken URL annotation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix code review agent timeouts caused by gpt-5.4 (#126783)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR was developed with Copilot assistance based on analysis of
workflow run logs and duration data.

## Problem

~20% of custom code review agent runs hit the 20-minute workflow
timeout. Analysis of all 43 timeout runs from the last 1000 workflow
executions shows:

- **93% of timeouts** are caused by GPT-5.4 sub-agents that never return
- GPT-5.4 is present in **100% of timeout runs** (24/24 checked in
detail)
- GPT-5.2-only runs have **0 timeouts** in 6+ successful runs
- 86% of timed-out runs had already posted the review — they time out
waiting for hung sub-agents
- Each timeout causes the agent job to fail, making the overall workflow
**red in CI** (even though the `conclusion` job succeeds) — PR authors
must manually rerun

The current SKILL.md rule "pick the highest version number" causes
gpt-5.4 to always be selected when available.

## Changes (SKILL.md only)

1. **Block gpt-5.4** — it has known reliability issues. Recommend
`gpt-5.3-codex` as the GPT-family pick instead. If that also exhibits
hangs, we can block the GPT family entirely with no expected quality
loss.
2. **Exit after posting** — the agent was lingering 2-3 minutes after
successfully posting the review comment, waiting for hung sub-agents.
Now it exits immediately once the comment is visible.
3. **Reduce max sub-agents from 4 to 3** — with only 2-3 model families
available in practice, 4 was never fully utilized.

## What this does NOT change

- The 10-minute sub-agent timeout instruction (already in place,
appropriate for agents that do return)
- The overall workflow `timeout-minutes: 20` (hardcoded in the compiled
`.lock.yml`)
- The review methodology, severity definitions, or quality bar
- Any CCR (Copilot Code Review) configuration

## Expected impact

- Eliminates the dominant timeout cause (GPT-5.4 hangs)
- Saves 2-3 min per run from exit-after-post
- No expected quality regression: GPT contributed unique blocking
findings in 0% of sampled runs

## Data

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Runs analyzed | 1000 workflow runs, 420 non-skipped, 218 with CLI data
|
| Timeout rate | 19.7% (43/218) |
| GPT-5.4 in timeouts | 100% (24/24 detailed) |
| GPT-5.2 timeouts | 0% (0/6+ successful GPT-5.2 runs) |
| Reviews with GPT-unique findings | <8% |
| GPT-only blocking bugs found | 0 |
| MCP add_comment missing | 12/43 timeout runs (~6% of all runs) —
platform issue, not addressed here |

## Why not increase the 20-minute timeout?

The GPT-5.4 sub-agent hangs indefinitely — there is no evidence it would
eventually complete if given more time. Increasing the timeout would
just delay the inevitable and waste more compute.

## Duration distribution (218 runs with CLI data)

| Bucket | Runs | % |
|--------|------|---|
| 0–2m | 18 | 8.3% |
| 2–4m | 24 | 11.0% |
| 4–6m | 29 | 13.3% |
| 6–8m | 25 | 11.5% |
| 8–10m | 26 | 11.9% |
| 10–12m | 21 | 9.6% |
| 12–14m | 9 | 4.1% |
| 14–16m | 8 | 3.7% |
| 16–18m | 9 | 4.1% |
| 18–20m | 6 | 2.8% |
| 20m+ (timeout) | 43 | 19.7% |

The bimodal distribution (healthy hump at 4–10m, spike at 20m wall)
confirms these are hangs, not slow completions.

Related: #126779 (not a fix for that, but general efficiency improvement
to the review agent)

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve interfaces→domain import violations in 4 notion subdomains

Create application-layer DTO re-export files for authoring, collaboration,
database, and knowledge subdomains. Update 26 interfaces/ files to import
domain types from application/dto/ instead of domain/ directly, enforcing
the hexagonal dependency direction: interfaces → application → domain.

New files:
- authoring/application/dto/authoring.dto.ts
- collaboration/application/dto/collaboration.dto.ts
- database/application/dto/database.dto.ts
- knowledge/application/dto/knowledge.dto.ts

commit.message:
Fix dashboard layout: resolve scoped CSS conflict with dashboard.css

MainLayout.razor.css (scoped) set sidebar to sticky/250px while
dashboard.css set it to fixed/260px with margin-left on main. The
scoped CSS won due to higher specificity (attribute selectors), causing
double-offset and wasted space. Now both use consistent sticky sidebar
at 260px with main flex:1 filling remaining space naturally.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

infrastructure issues:

1. Missing SSR content in fixture HTML: Both fixture.html and
   fixture-shadow.html had empty <test-module-host> elements. The shadow
   DOM fixture was also missing the declarative shadow root with
   shadowrootadoptedstylesheets. Without SSR content the framework
   treated the elements as client-created instead of hydrating them.

2. esbuild stripping import attributes: The fixture build used
   target es2022 without opting into import-attributes support. esbuild
   silently removed { with: { type: 'css' } } from dynamic import()
   calls in the compiled bundle, causing the browser to reject CSS
   module imports as JavaScript (MIME type mismatch).

3. Missing async wait in test assertions: injectModuleStyle uses
   import().then() to adopt stylesheets on dynamically-created
   components. The test checked computed styles synchronously before
   the promise resolved. Switched to Playwright toPass() polling.

Also remove the injectModuleStyle Node.js unit tests — they mocked
DOM queries for an implementation that uses browser-only CSS module
imports. The Playwright E2E tests provide real browser coverage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: webui
repository.description: WebUI is a language-agnostic server-side rendering framework that compiles HTML templates into a binary protocol at build time and uses Web Components Islands Architecture for client-side interactivity. commit.message:
Feat: sistema centralizado de debug logging (logs/debug.log)

- core/logger.py: novo módulo com setup_logging()
  * logs/hunt3r.log → INFO+ formato simples (comportamento existente)
  * logs/debug.log  → DEBUG+ com filename:line funcName() + tracebacks
  * RotatingFileHandler: 10MB por arquivo, 3 backups (debug.log.1-3)
  * _AutoExcInfoHandler: captura traceback ativo automaticamente em
    qualquer WARNING/ERROR dentro de except — sem precisar mudar
    nenhuma chamada existente de logging.error()
  * sys.excepthook: exceções não capturadas vão para debug.log
  * threading.excepthook: crashes em worker threads capturados também

- core/ui.py: substitui logging.basicConfig() por setup_logging()
- core/filter.py, core/ml_filter.py, core/updater.py, recon/platforms.py,
  main.py: marcados como no-op (os basicConfig(ERROR) já eram ignorados
  pelo Python depois do primeiro basicConfig, agora ficam explícitos)

The hook now walks up the directory tree from both cwd and file path

supports permissionDecision deny to block tool execution.

Update ApiChief baselines for MEAI and MEVD (#7461)

* Update ApiChief baselines for MEAI and MEVD

Ran '/run-apichief' skill for MEAI and MEVD.

Noteworthy changes for reviewers:

- MEVD.Abstractions PublicKeyToken changed from 31bf3856ad364e35
  (Microsoft strong name key) to f300afd708cefcd3. This differs from
  MEVD.ConformanceTests and all MEAI assemblies, which still use the
  original key. This reflects the assembly as-built and is not a
  baseline editing artifact.

- MEVD.Abstractions: CollectionModelBuildingOptions.CollectionModelBuildingOptions()
  stage changed from Experimental to Obsolete. The C# compiler emits
  [Obsolete] on parameterless constructors of types with 'required'
  members to protect older compilers. ApiChief's ParsedMember handles
  the analogous compiler-generated [Obsolete] for readonly ref structs
  but doesn't yet have a carve-out for the required-members variant,
  so it reports Obsolete instead of inheriting the parent type's
  Experimental stage.

- MEVD.Abstractions: 'where TRecord : class' generic constraints now
  appear on IKeywordHybridSearchable<TRecord>,
  IVectorSearchable<TRecord>, and VectorSearchResult<TRecord>.

- MEVD.Abstractions: Primary constructor portion removed from
  PropertyModel Type field (ILSpy decompiler limitation, issue
  icsharpcode/ILSpy#829 still open). Instruction comments added.

- MEVD.ConformanceTests: New baseline file (2597 lines). Primary
  constructor portions removed from 14 abstract test class Type fields
  with instruction comments.

- MEAI.Abstractions: ~70 new types added including Realtime client
  abstractions (IRealtimeClient, IRealtimeClientSession,
  RealtimeClientMessage, RealtimeServerMessage and subtypes),
  ITextToSpeechClient, ISpeechToTextClient, IHostedFileClient,
  HostedFileSearchTool, HostedImageGenerationTool, HostedMcpServerTool,
  and related content types. ~27 types removed/replaced.

- MEAI: ~25 new types for Realtime, TextToSpeech, and HostedFile
  delegating clients, builders, logging, and OpenTelemetry wrappers.

- MEAI.OpenAI: New OpenAIRealtimeClient, OpenAIRealtimeClientSession,
  and extension methods for AsIHostedFileClient/AsITextToSpeechClient.

- MEAI: 'scoped' replaced with 'params' on 4 ReadOnlySpan<object?>
  parameters (ILSpy decompiler limitation, icsharpcode/ILSpy#829).
  Instruction comments restored.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Exclude MEVD.ConformanceTests from ApiChief

* Override CollectionModelBuildingOptions stage to Experimental

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Evolve: Nuclei crash detection, tag validation e ML feedback loop

- engines.py: detecta returncode != 0 com output vazio → RuntimeError (crash
  silencioso agora visível como falha real no _run_with_progress)
- engines.py: re-raise exceptions de Nuclei para que scanner marque ok=False
- tech_detector.py: VALID_NUCLEI_TAGS whitelist com 50+ tags do community repo
  TAG_ALIASES mapeia tags internas inválidas (wpscan, token-crack, etc.) para
  equivalentes reais; fallback para cve,misconfig,takeover se nenhuma válida
- scanner.py: AI prompt enriquecido com severity, matched-at, description e
  extracted results para melhor qualidade de triage
- scanner.py: ML feedback loop — cada decisão AI (VALID/INVALID) salva em
  data/findings_feedback.jsonl para retreinamento do LightGBM

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
